descriptions: Plan the feature and confirm the approach with the user before writing any code.
Map out all the files you'll need to read or modify; check for reference implementations.
Repeat the plan back to the user and gate on explicit approval before any code is written.
Prepare the project environment and dependencies for prototyping.
Wire up API routes and seed the database with realistic, demo-rich data.
Iterate on the prototype with the live preview — navigate, edit, verify.
Verify the prototype's routes, images, and client errors before presenting.
Confirm or switch the workflow mode before the demo.
Present the live preview and get user approval to proceed.
Read the code and plan all component and function extractions.
Extract components and functions via TDD; cover library functions with multiple tests.
Register every new test and glossary entry produced in the prior step.
Rebuild the dependency graph after extraction.
Create isolation routes for every visual component.
Register and capture component-level scenarios for every visual component.
Curate the existing app-scenario set — review, consolidate, dedup, and edit in place — before registering new scenarios.
Create app-level scenarios that show full pages with realistic data.
Final approval gate — recapture screenshots and present a summary of all work to the user.
Run the audit gate, reconcile any registry/glossary drift until clean.
Create or update the journal entry documenting what was built.
Sync local state with the remote so the next commit lands on a merged tree.
Finalize the session — run the full suite once.
Commit all changes to git.
Push the branch to the remote repository.
Fire the feature-complete modal — the cycle ends here.
restrictions: Do NOT write any code. Plan, ask clarifying questions, confirm scope. Plan auto-advances — approval lives at the confirm-plan slug.
Read-only. Map files and check for reusable implementations. Run independent reads in parallel batches.
Wait for explicit user approval. Do NOT advance until the user picks `Approve`.
Do NOT scaffold from scratch (`create-next-app`, `npx create-*`, `vite create`). Use the existing project.
Wire API routes and seed realistic mock data only. Do NOT build features beyond what the data demands.
Iterate visually via the live preview. Speed of feedback only — tests come later.
Run the verification commands in parallel. Fix only — do NOT add features.
Confirm whether to keep the current workflow mode or switch via `editor switch-mode`. Do NOT modify code in this step.
Present (AskUserQuestion) and gate on user approval. Do NOT refactor or add tests until approved.
Read code and write the extraction plan. Do NOT write code yet. Read the glossary through the CLI, never with `Read`.
Extract via TDD — failing test first, then implement. Page files contain ZERO direct JSX.
Register every new test and glossary entry. Do NOT add new application code.
Rebuild the dependency graph. Read the graph and glossary through the CLI, never with `Read`.
Create isolation routes for every visual component. Do NOT modify component implementations.
Register and capture component scenarios only. Do NOT create app-level scenarios in this step.
Curate the existing app-scenario set — review, consolidate, dedup, retire obsolete, edit in place. Do NOT register new scenarios in this step.
Create app-level scenarios — do NOT set `componentName`. Do NOT modify application code.
Mandatory approval gate. Recapture screenshots, present, AskUserQuestion. Do NOT skip — reconcile fires AFTER this gate.
Run the audit gate in partial-run mode and reconcile drift. Do NOT pre-emptively run flag-free `refresh-tests`.

Create or update the journal entry. Do NOT include a timestamp — it is auto-generated. The journal entry's commit-SHA association is derived at read time from `git log --diff-filter=A` (see history-show-all-commits plan); do NOT write a `commitSha` field by hand.
Run `editor pre-commit-sync`. Do NOT skip — this is the enforced phase that catches remote drift and auto-recovers state.

Finalize: this branch is sticky Fast Commit. Run `codeyam-editor editor session-checkpoint --compile-only` (verify-build only; the full audit, tests, and finalize invariants are deferred to `session-finalize`). Do NOT run `session-finalize` — the user already chose to defer. If verify-build fails, fix the compile/lint error and re-run the same command. The deferred trailer is stamped on the feature commit at the Commit step via `mark-deferred`.
Commit all changes. Do NOT push — that happens at the push slug.
Check for a git remote and offer to push. WAIT for the user's answer before starting the next feature.
Fire the feature-complete modal. WAIT for the user's choice. Do NOT start a new feature without explicit instruction.

descriptions: Plan the feature and confirm the approach with the user before writing any code.
Map out all the files you'll need to read or modify; check for reference implementations.
Repeat the plan back to the user and gate on explicit approval before any code is written.
Prepare the project environment.
Build the feature. Tests come at the Deconstruct cluster.
Confirm or switch the workflow mode before the demo.
Show the prototype results and gate on the user's yes/iterate decision before any verification work.
Read the code and plan all function/method/helper extractions.
Extract functions/methods/helpers via TDD; every change must have a red-if-reverted test.
Register every new test and glossary entry produced in the prior step.
Rebuild the dependency graph after extraction.
Present the /results page with architecture diagram, tests, and changed files.
Run the audit gate, reconcile any registry/glossary drift until clean.
Author the session narrative against post-Verify code, then create the journal entry.
Sync local state with the remote so the next commit lands on a merged tree.
Finalize the session — run the full suite once.
Commit all changes to git.
Push the branch to the remote repository.
Fire the feature-complete modal — the cycle ends here.
restrictions: Do NOT write any code. Plan, ask clarifying questions, confirm scope. Plan auto-advances — approval lives at the confirm-plan slug.
Read-only. Map files and check for reusable implementations. Run independent reads in parallel batches.
Wait for explicit user approval. Do NOT advance until the user picks `Approve`.
Do NOT scaffold from scratch. Use the existing project.
Build the feature. Use `verify-build` for compile/lint feedback. Do NOT run `refresh-tests` — testing is deferred to the Deconstruct cluster (`backend-extract-tdd`).
Confirm whether to keep the current workflow mode or switch via `editor switch-mode`. Do NOT modify code in this step.
Show prototype results and gate on the user's yes/iterate decision. Do NOT proceed to verification until approved.
Read code and write the extraction plan. Do NOT write code yet. Read the glossary through the CLI, never with `Read`.
Extract functions/methods/helpers via TDD; every change must have a red-if-reverted test.
Register every new test and glossary entry. Do NOT add new application code.
Rebuild the dependency graph. Read the graph and glossary through the CLI, never with `Read`.
Present the /results page. Do NOT skip the approval gate.
Run the audit gate in partial-run mode and reconcile drift. Do NOT pre-emptively run flag-free `refresh-tests`.

Author the journal narrative against post-Verify code. The journal entry's commit-SHA association is derived at read time from `git log --diff-filter=A` (see history-show-all-commits plan); do NOT write a `commitSha` field by hand.
Run `editor pre-commit-sync`. Do NOT skip — this is the enforced phase that catches remote drift and auto-recovers state.

Finalize: this branch is sticky Fast Commit. Run `codeyam-editor editor session-checkpoint --compile-only` (verify-build only; the full audit, tests, and finalize invariants are deferred to `session-finalize`). Do NOT run `session-finalize` — the user already chose to defer. If verify-build fails, fix the compile/lint error and re-run the same command. The deferred trailer is stamped on the feature commit at the Commit step via `mark-deferred`.
Commit all changes. Do NOT push — that happens at the push slug.
Check for a git remote and offer to push. WAIT for the user's answer before starting the next feature.
Fire the feature-complete modal. WAIT for the user's choice. Do NOT start a new feature without explicit instruction.
